Adjustment of Annuities for Survivors of Certain Members Who Died While on Active Duty Between andSeptember 21, 1972November 29, 1990–(d), , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–189, div. A, title XIV, § 1403(b)103 Stat. 1579Nov. 29, 1989“(b)Adjustment of Annuities Already in Effect.—“(1).—RecomputationThe Secretary concerned shall recompute the annuity of any person who on the effective date specified in subsection (d) is entitled to an annuity under the Survivor Benefit Plan by reason of eligibility described in section 1448(d)(1)(B) or 1448(d)(1)(C) of title 10, United States Code, and who is further described in subsection (c).“(2).—Amount of recomputed annuitiesThe amount of the annuity as so recomputed shall be the amount that would be in effect for that annuity on the effective date specified in subsection (d) if the annuity had originally been computed subject to the provisions of paragraph (3) of , United States Code, as amended by subsection (a).section 1451(c) of title 10“(c).—Persons Eligible for RecomputationA person is eligible to have an annuity under the Survivor Benefit Plan recomputed under subsection (b) if—“(1)the annuity is based upon the service of a member of the uniformed services who died on active duty during the period beginning on , and ending on the effective date specified in subsection (d); andSeptember 21, 1972“(2)the retired pay of that member for the purposes of determining the amount of the annuity under the Survivor Benefit Plan was computed using a rate of basic pay lower than the rate of basic pay in effect at the time of death for the grade in which the member was serving at the time of death.“(d).—Effective DateAn annuity recomputed under subsection (b) shall take effect as so recomputed on .”March 1, 1990

Adjustment of Annuities for Survivors of Certain Members Who Died While on Active Duty Between andSeptember 21, 1972November 29, 1990–(d), , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–189, div. A, title XIV, § 1403(b)103 Stat. 1579Nov. 29, 1989“(b)Adjustment of Annuities Already in Effect.—“(1).—RecomputationThe Secretary concerned shall recompute the annuity of any person who on the effective date specified in subsection (d) is entitled to an annuity under the Survivor Benefit Plan by reason of eligibility described in section 1448(d)(1)(B) or 1448(d)(1)(C) of title 10, United States Code, and who is further described in subsection (c).“(2).—Amount of recomputed annuitiesThe amount of the annuity as so recomputed shall be the amount that would be in effect for that annuity on the effective date specified in subsection (d) if the annuity had originally been computed subject to the provisions of paragraph (3) of , United States Code, as amended by subsection (a).section 1451(c) of title 10“(c).—Persons Eligible for RecomputationA person is eligible to have an annuity under the Survivor Benefit Plan recomputed under subsection (b) if—“(1)the annuity is based upon the service of a member of the uniformed services who died on active duty during the period beginning on , and ending on the effective date specified in subsection (d); andSeptember 21, 1972“(2)the retired pay of that member for the purposes of determining the amount of the annuity under the Survivor Benefit Plan was computed using a rate of basic pay lower than the rate of basic pay in effect at the time of death for the grade in which the member was serving at the time of death.“(d).—Effective DateAn annuity recomputed under subsection (b) shall take effect as so recomputed on .”March 1, 1990

children.heading: Computation of Annuity for a Spouse, Former Spouse, or Child.—
children.label: a Computation of Annuity for a Spouse, Former Spouse, or Child.—

children.heading: .—Beneficiary under 62 years of age
children.label: A .—Beneficiary under 62 years of age
children.text: If the beneficiary is under 62 years of age or is a dependent child when becoming entitled to the annuity, the monthly annuity shall be the amount equal to 55 percent of the base amount.

children.heading: Beneficiary 62 years of age or older.—
children.label: B Beneficiary 62 years of age or older.—
I
children.text: For a month before October 2005, the applicable percent is 35 percent.

children.label: II
children.text: For months after September 2005 and before April 2006, the applicable percent is 40 percent.

children.label: III
children.text: For months after March 2006 and before April 2007, the applicable percent is 45 percent.

children.label: IV
children.text: For months after March 2007 and before April 2008, the applicable percent is 50 percent.

children.label: V
children.text: For months after March 2008, the applicable percent is 55 percent.

children.heading: .—Rule if beneficiary eligible for social security offset computation
children.label: ii .—Rule if beneficiary eligible for social security offset computation
children.text: If the beneficiary is eligible to have the annuity computed under subsection (e) and if computation of the annuity under that subsection is more favorable to the beneficiary than computation under clause (i), the annuity shall be computed under that subsection rather than under clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: is less than 55 percent; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is determined under subsection (f).

children.heading: Beneficiary 62 years of age or older.—
children.label: B Beneficiary 62 years of age or older.—
I
children.text: is less than the percent specified under subsection (a)(1)(B)(i) as being applicable for the month; and

children.label: II
children.text: is determined under subsection (f).

children.heading: .—Rule if beneficiary eligible for social security offset computation
children.label: ii .—Rule if beneficiary eligible for social security offset computation
children.text: If the beneficiary is eligible to have the annuity computed under subsection (e) and if, at the time the beneficiary becomes entitled to the annuity, computation of the annuity under that subsection is more favorable to the beneficiary than computation under clause (i), the annuity shall be computed under that subsection rather than under clause (i).

children.heading: .—Standard annuity
children.label: 1 .—Standard annuity
children.text: In the case of a standard annuity provided to a beneficiary under , the monthly annuity payable to the beneficiary shall be the amount equal to 55 percent of the retired pay of the person who elected to provide the annuity after the reduction in that pay in accordance with .section 1450(a)(5) of this titlesection 1452(c) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is less than 55 percent; and

children.label: B
children.text: is determined under subsection (f).

children.heading: .—Computation of reserve-component annuity when participant dies before age 60
children.label: 3 .—Computation of reserve-component annuity when participant dies before age 60
A
children.text: who provides an annuity that is determined in accordance with that paragraph;

children.label: B
children.text: who dies before becoming 60 years of age; and

children.label: C
children.text: who at the time of death is otherwise entitled to retired pay,

children.heading: Annuities for Survivors of Certain Persons Dying During a Period of Special Eligibility for SBP.—
children.label: c Annuities for Survivors of Certain Persons Dying During a Period of Special Eligibility for SBP.—
i
children.text: In the case of an annuity provided under section 1448(d) or 1448(f) of this title (other than in a case covered by clause (ii) or (iii)), such retired pay shall be computed as if the member had been retired under on the date of the member’s death with a disability rated as total.section 1201 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: In the case of an annuity provided under by reason of the death of a member not in line of duty, such retired pay shall be computed based upon the member’s years of active service when he died.section 1448(d)(1)(A) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: In the case of an annuity provided under by reason of the death of a member or former member not in line of duty, such retired pay shall be computed based upon the member or former member’s years of service when he died computed under .section 1448(f)(1)(A) of this titlesection 12733 of this title

children.heading: Beneficiary 62 years of age or older.—
children.label: B Beneficiary 62 years of age or older.—

children.heading: .—General rule
children.label: i .—General rule
children.text: If the person receiving the annuity (other than a dependent child) is 62 years of age or older when the member or former member dies, the monthly annuity shall be the amount equal to the applicable percent of the retired pay to which the member or former member would have been entitled as determined under subparagraph (A). The percent applicable for a month under the preceding sentence is the percent specified under subsection (a)(1)(B)(i) as being applicable for that month.

children.heading: .—Rule if beneficiary eligible for social security offset computation
children.label: ii .—Rule if beneficiary eligible for social security offset computation
children.text: If the beneficiary is eligible to have the annuity computed under subsection (e) and if computation of the annuity under that subsection is more favorable to the beneficiary than computation under clause (i), the annuity shall be computed under that subsection rather than under clause (i).

children.heading: .—DIC offset
children.label: 2 .—DIC offset
children.text: An annuity computed under paragraph (1) that is paid to a surviving spouse shall be reduced by a portion (calculated under ) of the amount of dependency and indemnity compensation to which the surviving spouse is entitled under . Any such reduction shall be effective on the date of the commencement of the period of payment of such compensation under title 38.section 1450(c) of this titlesection 1311(a) of title 38

children.heading: .—Servicemembers not yet granted retired pay
children.label: 3 .—Servicemembers not yet granted retired pay
children.text: In the case of an annuity provided by reason of the service of a member described in clause (ii) or (iii) of who first became a member of a uniformed service before , the retired pay to which the member would have been entitled when he died shall be determined for purposes of paragraph (1) based upon the rate of basic pay in effect at the time of death for the grade in which the member was serving at the time of death, unless (as determined by the Secretary concerned) the member would have been entitled to be retired in a higher grade.section 1448(d)(1)(A) of this titleSeptember 8, 1980

children.heading: .—Rate of pay to be used in computing annuity
children.label: 4 .—Rate of pay to be used in computing annuity
children.text: In the case of an annuity paid under by reason of the service of a person who first became a member of a uniformed service before , the retired pay of the person providing the annuity shall for the purposes of paragraph (1) be computed on the basis of the rates of basic pay in effect on the effective date of the annuity.section 1448(f) of this titleSeptember 8, 1980

children.heading: .—Reduction required
children.label: 1 .—Reduction required
children.text: The annuity of a person whose annuity is computed under subparagraph (A) of subsection (a)(1), (a)(2), or (c)(1) shall be reduced on the first day of the month after the month in which the person becomes 62 years of age.

children.heading: .—Computation of annuity
children.label: A .—Computation of annuity
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the reduced amount of the annuity shall be the amount of the annuity that the person would be receiving on that date if the annuity had initially been computed under subparagraph (B) of that subsection.

children.heading: .—Savings provision for beneficiaries eligible for social security offset computation
children.label: B .—Savings provision for beneficiaries eligible for social security offset computation
children.text: In the case of a person eligible to have an annuity computed under subsection (e) and for whom, at the time the person becomes 62 years of age, the annuity computed with a reduction under subsection (e)(3) is more favorable than the annuity with a reduction described in subparagraph (A), the reduction in the annuity shall be computed in the same manner as a reduction under subsection (e)(3).

children.heading: Savings Provision for Certain Beneficiaries.—
children.label: e Savings Provision for Certain Beneficiaries.—
A
children.text: A beneficiary receiving an annuity under the Plan on , as the surviving spouse or former spouse of the person providing the annuity.October 1, 1985

children.label: i
children.text: was a participant in the Plan;

children.label: ii
children.text: was entitled to retired pay or was qualified for that pay except that he had not applied for and been granted that pay; or

children.label: iii
children.text: would have been eligible for reserve-component retired pay but for the fact that he was under 60 years of age.

children.heading: .—Standard annuity
children.label: A .—Standard annuity
children.text: In the case of the beneficiary of a standard annuity, the annuity shall be the amount equal to 55 percent of the base amount.

children.label: i
children.text: is less than 55 percent; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is determined under subsection (f).

children.heading: .—Beneficiaries of persons dying during a period of special eligibility for sbp
children.label: C .—Beneficiaries of persons dying during a period of special eligibility for sbp
children.text: In the case of the beneficiary of an annuity under section 1448(d) or 1448(f) of this title, the annuity shall be the amount equal to 55 percent of the retired pay of the person providing the annuity (as that pay is determined under subsection (c)).

children.heading: .—Social security computation
children.label: A .—Social security computation
children.text: The amount of the survivor benefit, if any, to which the surviving spouse (or the former spouse, in the case of a former spouse beneficiary who became a former spouse under a divorce that became final after ) would be entitled under title II of the Social Security Act ( et seq.) based solely upon service by the person concerned as described in section 210()(1) of such Act (()(1)) and calculated assuming that the person concerned lives to age 65.November 29, 198942 U.S.C. 40142 U.S.C. 410ll

children.heading: .—Maximum amount of reduction
children.label: B .—Maximum amount of reduction
children.text: 40 percent of the amount of the monthly annuity as determined under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Special rules for social security offset computation.—
children.label: 4 Special rules for social security offset computation.—

children.heading: .—Treatment of deductions made on account of work
children.label: A .—Treatment of deductions made on account of work
children.text: For the purpose of paragraph (3), a surviving spouse (or a former spouse, in the case of a person who becomes a former spouse under a divorce that becomes final after ) shall not be considered as entitled to a benefit under title II of the Social Security Act ( et seq.) to the extent that such benefit has been offset by deductions under section 203 of such Act () on account of work.November 29, 198942 U.S.C. 40142 U.S.C. 403

children.heading: .—Treatment of certain periods for which social security refunds are made
children.label: B .—Treatment of certain periods for which social security refunds are made
i
children.text: which was performed after ; andDecember 1, 1980

children.label: ii
children.text: which involved periods of service of less than 30 continuous days for which the person concerned is entitled to receive a refund under section 6413(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 of the social security tax which the person had paid.

children.heading: .—Determination of Percentages Applicable to Computation of Reserve-Component Annuities
children.label: f .—Determination of Percentages Applicable to Computation of Reserve-Component Annuities

children.text: The age of the person electing to provide the annuity at the time of such election.
The difference in age between such person and the beneficiary of the annuity.
Whether such person provided for the annuity to become effective (in the event he died before becoming 60 years of age) on the day after his death or on the 60th anniversary of his birth.
Appropriate group annuity tables.
Such other factors as the Secretary considers relevant.

children.heading: .—Increases in annuities when retired pay increased
children.label: A .—Increases in annuities when retired pay increased
children.text: Whenever retired pay is increased under (or any other provision of law), each annuity that is payable under the Plan shall be increased at the same time.section 1401a of this title

children.heading: .—Percentage of increase
children.label: B .—Percentage of increase
children.text: The increase shall, in the case of any annuity, be by the same percent as the percent by which the retired pay of the person providing the annuity would have been increased at such time if the person were alive (and otherwise entitled to such pay).

children.heading: .—Certain reductions to be disregarded
children.label: C .—Certain reductions to be disregarded
children.text: The amount of the increase shall be based on the monthly annuity payable before any reduction under or under subsection (c)(2).section 1450(c) of this title

children.heading: .—Rounding down
children.label: 2 .—Rounding down
children.text: The monthly amount of an annuity payable under this subchapter, if not a multiple of $1, shall be rounded to the next lower multiple of $1.

children.heading: .—Increases in base amount when retired pay increased
children.label: A .—Increases in base amount when retired pay increased
children.text: Whenever retired pay is increased under (or any other provision of law), the base amount applicable to each participant in the Plan shall be increased at the same time.section 1401a of this title

children.heading: .—Percentage of increase
children.label: B .—Percentage of increase
children.text: The increase shall be by the same percent as the percent by which the retired pay of the participant is so increased.

children.heading: .—Recomputation at age 62
children.label: 2 .—Recomputation at age 62
children.text: When the retired pay of a person who first became a member of a uniformed service on or after , and who is a participant in the Plan is recomputed under upon the person’s becoming 62 years of age, the base amount applicable to that person shall be recomputed (effective on the effective date of the recomputation of such retired pay under ) so as to be the amount equal to the amount of the base amount that would be in effect on that date if increases in such base amount under paragraph (1) had been computed as provided in paragraph (2) of (rather than under paragraph (3) of that section).August 1, 1986section 1410 of this titlesection 1410 of this titlesection 1401a(b) of this title

children.heading: .—Disregarding of retired pay reductions for retirement of certain members before 30 years of service
children.label: 3 .—Disregarding of retired pay reductions for retirement of certain members before 30 years of service
children.text: Computation of a member’s retired pay for purposes of this section shall be made without regard to any reduction under .section 1409(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: .—Recomputation of Annuity for Certain Beneficiaries
children.label: i .—Recomputation of Annuity for Certain Beneficiaries
children.text: In the case of an annuity under the Plan which is computed on the basis of the retired pay of a person who would have been entitled to have that retired pay recomputed under upon attaining 62 years of age, but who dies before attaining that age, the annuity shall be recomputed, effective on the first day of the first month beginning after the date on which the member or former member would have attained 62 years of age, so as to be the amount equal to the amount of the annuity that would be in effect on that date if increases under subsection (h)(1) in the base amount applicable to that annuity to the time of the death of the member or former member, and increases in such annuity under subsection (g)(1), had been computed as provided in paragraph (2) of (rather than under paragraph (3) of that section).section 1410 of this titlesection 1401a(b) of this title
